kept.text:
BREAKING: SpaceX's board has approved a new compensation package for Elon Musk ahead of its record-setting IPO.

The package includes:

1. 200 million super-voting shares if SpaceX hits a $7.5 trillion valuation and establishes a permanent Mars colony with at least 1 million people 

2. Additional 60.4 million shares if SpaceX reaches certain valuation goals and operates data centers in space delivering 100 terawatts of compute

3. Elon Musk will not receive a single share if the company fails to reach the board's valuation targets

Elon Musk has received a salary from SpaceX of $54,080 per year since 2019.

kept.text:
Massive win for SpaceX and Elon Musk 🔥

The California Coastal Commission just formally apologized to Elon Musk and SpaceX, settling their October 2024 lawsuit

Previously, the Commission tried to block SpaceX launches at Vandenberg Space Force Base. Rather than citing safety or environmental reasons, commissioners openly complained about Elon’s politics and they didn’t like his posts on 𝕏

In the settlement: 
→ The Commission admitted to political bias during the October 2024 hearings 
→ They legally agreed to drop politics and speech from future regulatory decisions 
→ SpaceX gets long-term regulatory clarity and no longer needs coastal permits for Falcon launches at Vandenberg

A massive victory for free speech and this clears the path for an unstoppable launch cadence

NEWS: SpaceX’s board has approved Elon Musk’s new compensation package

- 200 million super-voting shares if they hit a $7.5 trillion valuation and establish a permanent Mars colony with at least 1 million people
- Another ~60 million shares if they operate data centers in space delivering 100 terawatts of compute (that’s the equivalent of 100,000 nuclear reactors)

https://t.co/l7ip8WsMkR

kept.text:
Starlink is absolutely DOMINATING satellite broadband

Here’s the April 2026 snapshot:
• Starlink (SpaceX): ~10,300 active LEO satellites 
• Eutelsat OneWeb: ~650 
• Amazon Kuiper: ~240 
• Viasat & HughesNet: ~ handful each (GEO) 
• Telesat Lightspeed: 0

Starlink has more satellites than every competitor combined....by a ridiculous margin 🔥

~11.8 million subscribers worldwide

Best combo of speed (50–400+ Mbps), latency (20–50 ms), and true global coverage

The gap is massive. Competition is coming… but not anytime soon

Starlink is rewriting the rules of global connectivity 🌍

The California Coastal Commission apologized to Elon Musk and SpaceX, admitting bias and pledging not to consider political views in future decisions

The settlement also confirms no coastal permits will be required for most Falcon 9 launches at Vandenberg Space Force Base https://t.co/bmtPlz5nH9

kept.text:
I approve Elon being the only person that can fire Elon - and it’s true.

According to reports, the confidential SpaceX IPO S-1 sets up a dual-class share structure where public investors get Class A shares (1 vote each). Elon and insiders control the Class B super-voting shares (10 votes each). And the filing explicitly says Elon can only be removed as CEO, Chairman, or from the board by a vote of the Class B holders.

So in practice, only Elon can fire himself. This is because he will control the vast majority of the Class B super-voting shares. Importantly, it’s not because he owns roughly ~42% of the total equity - it’s the 10x voting power of those Class B shares that gives him exclusive control over decisions like firing himself as CEO or Chairman.

This protects the long-term mission from short-term Wall Street pressure. As a long-term SpaceX investor, this makes total sense to me.

kept.text:
GOOD NEWS 🚨 Only Elon Musk can fire @elonmusk from SpaceX 🚀

According to the IPO filing, Musk "can only be removed from our board or these positions by the vote of Class B holders." Because he will control these super-voting shares—which carry ten votes each—after the IPO, removing him essentially requires his own approval. 

The filing adds that if he "retains a significant portion of his holdings of Class B common stock for an extended period of time, he could continue to control the election and removal of a majority of our board."

kept.text:
【CZ 自传 008｜五万年薪、火星殖民地、让我才看懂CZ，也问住了自己】

马斯克从 SpaceX 领的年薪是多少？

54080 美元。

看到这个数字的时候以为自己看错了。一个身价三千亿的人，年薪五万出头。比湾区一个刚毕业的初级程序员都低。

但后面那个条件更离谱，SpaceX 董事会批准的薪酬方案是：如果公司估值达到 7.5 万亿，并且在火星建立一个 100 万人的永久殖民地，马斯克将获得 2 亿股超级投票权股票。

火星。一百万人。永久殖民地。

他把自己的全部回报，押在了一件大部分人觉得这辈子都不可能发生的事上。

看完这条消息之后，立刻去翻书。因为我突然想起来书里有几个细节。

以前读的时候没什么感觉，但今天跟马斯克这条新闻放在一起，一下子就通了。

CZ 第一次创业做富讯，头两年合伙人没领过一分钱工资。他把在彭博攒的大约 20 万美元几乎是全部积蓄，全部投进公司运营。后来在东京做自己的交易所，拿存款给团队发工资，自己一分不拿。

一分不拿。

这四个字我以前读的时候划过去了。今天再看，突然觉得不一样了。

@heyibinance 在序言里写过一段，他被捧成全球最大加密交易平台的创始人之后，还是穿着亚马逊的平价衣服，骑自行车去开会，跟记者炫耀他那辆丰田面包车能调节座椅。因为他做了两次脊椎手术。记者写成段子嘲笑。他不生气。

马斯克年薪 5 万美元。CZ 创业时一分不拿。一个穿平价衣服，一个睡工厂。

今天之前我觉得这是“大佬的故事”，听听就好。但今天这两件事放在一起的时候，我突然意识到它们在讲同一件事。而且这件事跟我们有关。

马斯克把自己的回报绑在火星上。
--  不是绑在 SpaceX 的股价上，
--  不是绑在今年的财报上，
--  是绑在一个“火星 100 万人”的条件上。

他每天做的每一个决策，都不是在优化“今年多赚多少钱”。而是在优化“火星这件事能不能成”。

所以他可以年薪 5 万，可以睡工厂，可以被全网骂，因为这些事跟火星无关。它们动摇不了他。

@cz_binance 把自己的回报绑在什么上面？他在 AMA 上说过，Freedom of Money，核心是 access——让普通人能接触到金融工具。

他出狱之后做的第一件事不是赚钱，是做 Giggle Academy，一个免费教育项目。何一问他为什么，他说“因为我是教育的受益者”。

一个刚坐完四个月牢的人，出来做的第一件事是免费教育。

然后我问了自己一个问题。问完之后我沉默了很久。

我把自己的回报绑在什么上面？

答案太清楚了 —— 币价。

BTC 涨了，我觉得我做对了，觉得这个行业前途无量，觉得我的坚持有意义。

BTC 跌了，我开始怀疑，怀疑自己的判断，怀疑这个行业还有没有未来，怀疑是不是该去做点别的。

我的信心、我的情绪、我对这个行业的所有感受。全部跟着一根 K 线走。K 线涨我是信仰者，K 线跌我是怀疑者。

我以为这叫长期主义。我在这个行业待了六年，穿越了两轮牛熊。我跟别人说“我是长期的”。

但今天看完马斯克和 CZ 之后，我突然意识到，我不是长期主义者。我只是一个还没卖的短期主义者。

因为我绑定的是价格。价格是短期的。它每天都在变。我的信心跟着它变，说明我的信心也是短期的。只是恰好到今天为止，价格还没有跌到让我彻底失去信心的那个点。

这跟马斯克绑在火星上、CZ 绑在 Freedom of Money 上，根本不是同一种东西。他们绑定的东西不会因为某一天跌了 10% 就消失。我绑定的东西每天都在消失和出现、消失和出现。

真正的长期主义不是“我还没卖”。

是“我知道我在等什么，而那件事不会因为价格波动而消失”。

马斯克在等火星。CZ 在等 Freedom of Money。

我在等什么？

这个问题我到现在没有完整的答案。
但我觉得能问出这个问题，本身就是读 CZ 这本书给我的最大收获，比任何一段故事、任何一条原则都大。

因为在这之前，我甚至不知道自己需要回答这个问题。

这本书我还在读。每次读到一些东西，就会停下来想很久。

profile_bio.description:
宏观周期 |  加密投研 |  美股研究 |  AI x Crypto
Cycles always win . Be early , be patient , be honest .

kept.text:
🇺🇸 SpaceX launched a Falcon Heavy and a Falcon 9 back to back today.

New record for shortest gap between Falcon family launches.

tweet.text:
Elon Musk will earn nothing from SpaceX unless humanity becomes a multi-planetary species.

That’s not a headline. That’s literally the compensation structure.

SpaceX has tied Musk’s pay to milestones that no other company has ever dared to put in a filing — a real human presence on Mars, computing infrastructure scaled across space, and a valuation of approximately $7.5 trillion.

No fixed salary. No base pay. No guaranteed anything.

Think about what that means. The man running the most valuable private company in history — about to go public in the largest IPO ever — has structured his personal financial future around whether or not we actually get to Mars.

Gwynne Shotwell wants a Moon settlement in 10 years. Trump said “next step, Mars.” Starship is on the pad. Terafab is building compute for space. 80% of Terafab’s output is going to orbit.

Every piece of the puzzle was always pointing at the same destination.

And now Elon has made it personal.
 
He doesn’t get paid unless we get there.
